stages.title: The 5 Stages of Bike Grief
stages.subtitle: Processing the loss of your two-wheeled companion.
stages.show_all: I'm ready. Show me all the stages.
stages.step_back: I need to take a step back.

stagesets.desc: "I probably just parked it on the next block. Or maybe it's invisible today."
stagesets.advice: Keep walking around the block in circles. It builds calf muscles.

stagesets.desc: "I hope the thief rides it into a volcano."
stagesets.advice: Scream into a pillow. Or write a strongly worded Yelp review for the sidewalk.

stagesets.desc: "Walking is natural. Humans have walked for millennia. I love walking. It takes 45 minutes to get milk now."
stagesets.advice: Invest in good shoes. You live in them now.

stagesets.desc: "$2.75? For a ride that smells like unwashed gym socks?"
stagesets.advice: Accept that public transit is your new chauffeur.

stagesets.desc: "It was a nice bike. It belongs to the universe now. (And also Craig from Craigslist)."
stagesets.advice: Buy a U-lock next time. A real one.

stagesets.desc: "Wow, the bike fairy must have needed my specialized hybrid for an emergency!"
stagesets.advice: Take deep breaths. The fairy isn't real.

stagesets.desc: "Why did they leave the front wheel? It's a very nice wheel."
stagesets.advice: Carry the wheel everywhere to remind you of your loss.

stagesets.desc: "If I post enough flyers, they'll surely return it with an apology note."
stagesets.advice: Use waterproof markers for the flyers. Tears will smudge them.

stagesets.desc: "Look at that guy riding a bike. Does he know how lucky he is?"
stagesets.advice: Stop glaring at cyclists. They can feel it.

stagesets.desc: "I am buying a $50 rusted frame from the 80s that nobody will touch."
stagesets.advice: Get a tetanus shot before your first ride.

stagesets.desc: "I locked it for literally 45 seconds while I grabbed a coffee."
stagesets.advice: Next time, bring the bike into the cafe. The barista will understand.

stagesets.desc: "I will become a vigilante hero who hunts bike thieves in the night."
stagesets.advice: Cancel the batarang order. You don't have the budget.

stagesets.desc: "Remember that time we rode through the park and hit a pothole together? Good times."
stagesets.advice: Write a poem about the pothole. It helps.

stagesets.desc: "I guess this city really does require a sacrifice every few months."
stagesets.advice: Pay your tribute and move on.

stagesets.desc: "I don't need gears or brakes. I'm getting a unicycle."
stagesets.advice: Also get knee pads. Lots of them.

services.title: Counseling Services
services.subtitle: Additional premium resources to aid your spiritual recovery.

obituary.title: Obituary Templates
obituary.body: Beautifully crafted, pre-written obituaries for your local classifieds. Honor the exact top-tube geometry and emotional impact your bike had on your life.

articles.title: Grief Articles
articles.body: Explore our peer-reviewed library dissecting the psychological impact of seeing a stranger riding your bike two weeks later and knowing there's nothing you can do.

medium.title: Personal Medium
medium.body: Connect with an automated online medium who can channel online the remaining spirit of your bicycle, helping you finally say goodbye to its ghost.
